# Full cmd2mcpserver probe test using mcp-probe
#
# This test demonstrates the complete workflow of converting
# a program to MCP server and probing it

# Create a simple program
-- greet/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

var who = flag.String("who", "world", "who to greet")

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	fmt.Printf("Hello, %s!\n", *who)
}

-- greet/go.mod --
module github.com/test/greet

go 1.21

# Use cmd2mcpserver to generate the MCP server
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver greet
stdout 'Server generated successfully'

# Build the MCP server
cd mcpserver
exec go build -o greet-mcp main.go
cd ..

# Create a simple probe script that sends initialize
-- probe_input.txt --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"0.1.0","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}},"id":1}

# Pipe the probe input to the MCP server and check response
stdin probe_input.txt
exec ./mcpserver/greet-mcp
stdout '"jsonrpc":\s*"2.0"'
stdout '"id":\s*1'
stdout '"result"'
stdout '"protocolVersion"'
stdout '"capabilities"'

# Check server info in response
stdout '"serverInfo"'
stdout '"name":\s*"greet-mcp"'
